message:
feat(runtime_compat): Cx-first send_with_cx on broadcast/oneshot (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extends the primitive-layer Cx-first migration from tick 9 (Mutex +
RwLock lock/read/write) to the channel senders and oneshot_recv
helper. Downstream modules that have a &Cx in hand can now send
through broadcast / oneshot channels without going through ambient
cx::for_request() indirection.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs (+95 source, +72 tests)

   a. `broadcast::Sender::send_with_cx(cx, value) -> Result<usize, SendError<T>>`

      New Cx-first entry point. Takes an explicit &Cx instead of
      grabbing one via `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`.
      Legacy `send()` delegates to send_with_cx with the ambient Cx
      so existing callers keep working unchanged.

   b. `oneshot::Sender::send_with_cx(self, cx, value) -> Result<(), T>`

      Same pattern. Consumes `self` because oneshot senders fire at
      most once. Legacy `send()` delegates.

   c. `oneshot_recv_with_cx(cx, rx) -> Result<T, String>`

      Cx-first companion to `oneshot_recv`. Legacy `oneshot_recv`
      delegates to this under `asupersync-runtime` so the two paths
      stay in lockstep. Tokio fallback is unchanged.

2. Four new LabRuntime tests under `labruntime_sync_primitives_cx`:

   * `broadcast_send_with_cx_delivers_under_live_cx` (seed 4005):
     happy path — sender reaches receiver under a live Cx.

   * `broadcast_send_with_cx_surfaces_send_error_when_no_receivers`
     (seed 4006): SendError(value) when receivers dropped — matches
     send() behavior.

   * `oneshot_send_with_cx_roundtrip_under_live_cx` (seed 4007):
     end-to-end send_with_cx + oneshot_recv_with_cx roundtrip.

   * `oneshot_send_with_cx_returns_value_when_receiver_dropped`
     (seed 4008): Err(value) on dropped receiver.

Impact: runtime_compat primitive layer now has Cx-first variants for
Mutex::lock, RwLock::read/write, broadcast::Sender::send,
oneshot::Sender::send, and oneshot_recv. Combined with pre-existing
Cx-first primitives (timeout_with_cx, sleep_with_cx, Semaphore::
acquire_with_cx, mpsc::recv_with_cx, watch::Receiver::changed taking
&Cx) the primitive layer is now coherently Cx-first — call-site
migration in higher-level modules can proceed without needing to
surface ambient-Cx plumbing every time.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib runtime_compat::: 192/192 pass
    (188 after tick 9 + 4 new LabRuntime)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
